framing: This interview guide explores individual pathways to refugee support volunteering, with a focus on both historical involvement in activism/volunteering and specific mobilization around Ukrainian refugee support in 2022. The questions aim to understand personal motivations, expectations, and the process of becoming involved in refugee support work.
introduction:
Thank you for agreeing to participate in this interview about your experiences supporting refugees. We're particularly interested in understanding how people like yourself became involved in refugee support work and what that experience has been like.

There are no right or wrong answers - we want to hear your personal story and perspectives. Feel free to take your time with your responses and share as much detail as you're comfortable with.
outro: Thank you so much for sharing your experiences with us. Your insights will help us better understand how people become involved in refugee support work and what motivates them to help others in need. Is there anything else you'd like to add that we haven't covered?

question_sections.description: This section explores the participant's history with activism and volunteering, including family influences and past experiences that may have shaped their current involvement in refugee support.

questions.main_question: Thinking as far back as you can, did you have any expectations or hopes of doing something like volunteering where you support others in need?
questions.description: Aims to understand early motivations and aspirations related to helping others
probes: What inspired these hopes?
How did these early ideas compare to your current volunteer work?

questions.main_question: In your upbringing was volunteering or refugee support something that family members participated in?
questions.description: Explores family influence and early exposure to volunteering
probes: What kinds of activities did they participate in?
How did their involvement influence your perspective?

questions.main_question: Can you take me back to your earlier experiences with activism or volunteering. What was the first cause or organization you got involved with?
probes: What motivated you to get involved?
What was that experience like for you?

questions.main_question: If you remember back to the 2015 refugee crisis, what was your immediate reaction?
questions.description: Examines response to previous refugee crisis for comparison
probes: Did you take any action at that time?
How does that compare to your reaction to the Ukrainian crisis?

question_sections.description: This section focuses specifically on the participant's involvement in Ukrainian refugee support in 2022, exploring their initial motivations, expectations, and the process of getting involved.

questions.main_question: Can you tell me about the moment when you first realized you wanted to help Ukrainian refugees. Was there a particular event, news story, or conversation that triggered it or something else?
probes: How did that make you feel?
What thoughts went through your mind at that moment?

questions.main_question: Did you have any expectations of who needed your help?
questions.description: Explores preconceptions about refugees and their needs
probes: Where did these expectations come from?
How did reality compare to your expectations?

questions.main_question: Can you describe who you imagined this person to be?
questions.description: Delves deeper into preconceptions and mental images
probes: What characteristics did you imagine?
What needs did you think they would have?

questions.main_question: Did you have any hopes for the type of help you would do?
probes: What skills did you think you could offer?
How did your actual role compare to what you imagined?

questions.main_question: After you realized that you wanted to help? Please walk me through the steps you took after deciding to get involved.
probes: What resources did you seek out?
What challenges did you face?

questions.main_question: Can you describe how you connected with the people or groups that are part of your refugee work?
questions.description: Explores network formation and organizational connections
probes: How did you find these groups?
What was your initial interaction like?

questions.main_question: Did you become a member of a Facebook refugee support group?
questions.description: Examines role of social media in mobilization
probes: What motivated you to join?
How active were you in the group?

questions.main_question: Remembering back to the first time you looked at the group online, what was your impression?
questions.description: Captures initial reactions to online refugee support communities
probes: What stood out to you?
How did this influence your decision to get involved?
